title: podman --network-backend netavark report Error in CentOS Stream 9
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Try podman with netavark in CentOS stream9, and get a error message:
Error: netavark: : EOF

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install podman and netavark from podman-next
```
# dnf copr enable rhcontainerbot/podman-next -y
# dnf install podman netavark -y
```
2. Start a container with --network-backend netavark
```
# podman --network-backend netavark  run -it quay.io/libpod/busybox
Error: netavark: : EOF
# dmesg
[525700.929135] podman0: port 1(veth76843789) entered blocking state
[525700.929143] podman0: port 1(veth76843789) entered disabled state
[525700.929234] device veth76843789 entered promiscuous mode
[525700.940427] IPv6: ADDRCONF(NETDEV_CHANGE): eth0: link becomes ready
[525700.940457] IPv6: ADDRCONF(NETDEV_CHANGE): veth76843789: link becomes ready
[525700.940556] podman0: port 1(veth76843789) entered blocking state
[525700.940560] podman0: port 1(veth76843789) entered forwarding state
[525700.944653] traps: netavark[58104] trap invalid opcode ip:55b1df6fe675 sp:7ffdd128ea60 error:0 in netavark[55b1df5e4000+342000]
[525701.034452] podman0: port 1(veth76843789) entered disabled state
[525701.036059] device veth76843789 left promiscuous mode
[525701.036070] podman0: port 1(veth76843789) entered disabled state

```


**Describe the results you received:**
podman report error message

**Describe the results you expected:**
container should can be start and network works as expected

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.0.0-dev
API Version:  4.0.0-dev
Go Version:   go1.17.2

Built:      Fri Dec  3 08:00:00 2021
OS/Arch:    linux/amd64
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**
```
# rpm -qa |grep -e podman -e netavark
podman-gvproxy-3.4.3-0.6.el9.x86_64
podman-plugins-3.4.3-0.6.el9.x86_64
podman-4-20211207.194059.x86_64
netavark-0-20211203.210831.x86_64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
I am running a CentOS Stream9 KVM guest in linux. Installed from the ISO download from [CentOS website](https://www.centos.org/centos-stream/).
Here is more information for my VM:
```
# uname -r
5.14.0-22.el9.x86_64
```
Qemu command line:
```
/usr/libexec/qemu-kvm \
    -S  \
    -device VGA,bus=pci.0,addr=0x2 \
    -device pci-bridge,id=pci_bridge,bus=pci.0,addr=0x3,chassis_nr=1  \
    -device virtio-scsi-pci,id=virtio_scsi_pci0,bus=pci.0,addr=0x7 \
    -drive id=drive_image1,if=none,snapshot=off,aio=threads,cache=unsafe,format=qcow2,file=c9.qcow2 \
    -device scsi-hd,id=image1,drive=drive_image1 \
    -device virtio-net-pci,mac=fa:af:ff:b0:e1:e6,id=id944f2,vectors=4,netdev=idgAs,bus=pci.0,addr=0x8  \
    -netdev tap,id=idgAs \
    -m 8192  \
    -smp 4,maxcpus=4,cores=2,threads=1,sockets=2 \
    -drive id=drive_cd1,if=none,snapshot=off,aio=threads,cache=unsafe,media=cdrom,file=CentOS-Stream-9-latest-x86_64-dvd1.iso \
    -device ide-drive,bus=ide.1,unit=0,drive=drive-ide0-1-0 \
    -vnc :59  \
    -rtc base=utc,clock=host  \
    -boot order=cdn,once=d,menu=off,strict=off  \
    -no-shutdown \
    -enable-kvm  \
    -watchdog i6300esb \
    -watchdog-action reset \
    -device virtio-balloon-pci,id=balloon0,bus=pci.0,addr=0x9 \
    -monitor stdio
```

comments.body: OK I just installed centos stream 9 in a VM and it works for me. Same podman and netavark package versions, the only difference I can see is that I am on kernel version `5.14.0-25.el9.x86_64`

Hi @Luap99 I update the system and now my kernel version is 5.14.0-25.el9.x86_64 now. But it still failed. Here is my network devices inside the vm:
```
# ip addr
1: lo: <LOOPBACK,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 65536 qdisc noqueue state UNKNOWN group default qlen 1000
    link/loopback 00:00:00:00:00:00 brd 00:00:00:00:00:00
    inet 127.0.0.1/8 scope host lo
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 ::1/128 scope host 
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
2: ens8: <BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 1500 qdisc fq_codel state UP group default qlen 1000
    link/ether fa:af:fe:b0:e1:e6 brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff
    altname enp0s8
    inet 10.73.3.149/23 brd 10.73.3.255 scope global dynamic noprefixroute ens8
       valid_lft 83669sec preferred_lft 83669sec
    inet6 2620:52:0:4902:f8af:feff:feb0:e1e6/64 scope global dynamic noprefixroute 
       valid_lft 2591901sec preferred_lft 604701sec
    inet6 fe80::f8af:feff:feb0:e1e6/64 scope link noprefixroute 
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
3: podman0: <NO-CARRIER,BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP> mtu 1500 qdisc noqueue state DOWN group default qlen 1000
    link/ether 00:00:00:00:00:00 brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff
    inet 10.88.0.1/16 brd 10.88.255.255 scope global podman0
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 fe80::60e4:d8ff:fee2:244b/64 scope link 
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever

```
If you need any more information for debugging the problem, please let me know.
I just pulled CentOS-Stream-GenericCloud-9-20211214.3.x86_64.qcow2 and followed the instructions provided by the reporter and it worked perfectly.
It's gotta be an environment issue - something about the CPU featureset the VM claims to have, maybe?
@ypu Can you share `cat /proc/cpuinfo`? I found some issues online where people had similar problems with a AMD processor.

Here is the cpuinfo from my vm:
```
# cat /proc/cpuinfo 
processor	: 0
vendor_id	: GenuineIntel
cpu family	: 6
model		: 13
model name	: QEMU Virtual CPU version 1.5.3
stepping	: 3

cache size	: 4096 KB

flags		: fpu de pse tsc msr pae mce cx8 apic sep mtrr pge mca cmov pse36 clflush mmx fxsr sse sse2 ht syscall nx lm rep_good nopl cpuid tsc_known_freq pni cx16 hypervisor lahf_lm abm pti
bugs		: cpu_meltdown spectre_v1 spectre_v2 spec_store_bypass l1tf mds swapgs itlb_multihit
bogomips	: 4799.99

....
```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Closing since we now have official packages it should work, if not please let us know.

I am seeing the same behavior (I describe it here: https://unix.stackexchange.com/questions/753840/podman1-port-1veth0-entered-disabled-blocking-state-podman-does-not-gracefu) 

Was there a specific resolution?
